title: QUIC client-side replacement peer Initial reset work item
summary: Add the narrow client-side pre-transcript reset boundary for replacement peer Initial packets that prove a new server attempt, while keeping same-attempt retransmissions, Retry ownership, and broader multi-attempt behavior out of scope.
planned_changes:
- Track the first accepted peer Initial source connection ID together with the first offset-zero Initial CRYPTO prefix while the client runtime is still establishing.
- When a later peer Initial changes both markers before the peer transcript completes, reset the current peer-handshake attempt state and reapply the replacement Initial through the normal Initial path.
- Clear buffered Initial ingress CRYPTO, transcript offsets, and derived Handshake/open material from the earlier peer attempt so the replacement attempt can rederive cleanly.
- Keep same-crypto source-connection-ID drift on the current attempt instead of resetting it.
- Add focused requirement-home coverage for positive replacement-attempt reset and negative same-crypto no-reset behavior, and preserve captured replay evidence for the multiconnect replacement-server path.
verification_plan: Run the focused requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-CRT-0122 and REQ-QUIC-CRT-0146 together with the captured multiconnect replay tests, rerun the helper-backed client-role `quic-go` `multiconnect` interop reproduction, then render and validate the SpecTrace markdown and JSON artifacts.
out_of_scope: Retry ownership or token lifecycle changes beyond REQ-QUIC-CRT-0122
opportunistic refactors unrelated to the pre-transcript reset boundary
completion_notes: Implemented the narrow client-side replacement peer Initial reset boundary, retained same-attempt retransmissions when the Initial CRYPTO prefix stays the same, added focused requirement-home coverage, and preserved captured replay evidence from the multiconnect replacement-server path.
